# Golden Fixture Files

This directory contains binary fixture files used by the golden-sample regression harness
(`tests/test_golden_regression.py`). These files are committed to the repository so
tests can run fully offline — no network fetch at test time.

## Directory layout

```
golden/
  pdf/    — PDF fixture files (*.pdf)
  docx/   — DOCX fixture files (*.docx) — .gitkeep placeholder until files added
  pptx/   — PPTX fixture files (*.pptx) — .gitkeep placeholder until files added
  README.md — this file
```

## Size ceiling

Each fixture file must be ≤ 200 KB (recommended: ≤ 50 KB for synthetic fixtures).
This ceiling keeps repository clone time and CI memory usage low.

## License / provenance

- `pdf/test.pdf` — synthetic fixture created by the project team; no third-party content.
- `pdf/simple.pdf` — synthetic single-page fixture; no third-party content.
- `pdf/multipage.pdf` — synthetic two-page fixture; no third-party content.

All files in this directory are either synthetically generated by the project team
or sourced from license-compatible public-domain / CC0 materials.
New fixtures must document their provenance here before being committed.

## Snapshot files

Each fixture may ship with a companion `*.ir.json` snapshot:
```json
{
  "element_count": <int>,
  "element_types": {"text": <int>, ...},
  "reading_order_present": <bool>
}
```
Snapshot files are written on first run if absent, and compared on subsequent runs.
A difference on any pre-existing field fails the gate; a `reading_order`-only change
(i.e., old snapshot predates this field) does not.

## Adding new fixtures

1. Add the file to the appropriate subdirectory.
2. Verify file size is ≤ 200 KB.
3. Document provenance in this README.
4. Run the golden test suite once to generate the snapshot: `pytest tests/test_golden_regression.py -v`.
5. Commit both the fixture file and its generated snapshot.
